questions.question: Which of the following expressions are polynomials in one variable and which are not? State reasons for your answer. **(i) $4x^2 - 3x + 7$**

solution.content: A polynomial in one variable is an expression of the form $a_nx^n + a_{n-1}x^{n-1} + \dots + a_1x + a_0$, where $a_i$ are constants and $n$ is a non-negative integer.
This expression contains only one variable $x$.
The powers of $x$ are 2, 1, and 0, all of which are non-negative integers.
Therefore, $4x^2 - 3x + 7$ **is** a polynomial in one variable $x$.

questions.answer: Yes, it is a polynomial in one variable $x$.

formulaBox.title: Definition of Polynomial in One Variable

questions.question: Write the coefficients of $x^2$ in each of the following: **(i) $2 + x^2 + x$**

solution.content: The coefficient of $x^2$ is the constant multiplied by $x^2$.

questions.question: Give one example each of a binomial of degree 35, and of a monomial of degree 100.

solution.content: A **binomial** is a polynomial with exactly two terms.
A binomial of degree 35 must have the highest power of the variable equal to 35.
Example of a binomial of degree 35: **$x^{35} + 1$**
A **monomial** is a polynomial with exactly one term.
A monomial of degree 100 must have the highest power of the variable equal to 100.
Example of a monomial of degree 100: **$x^{100}$** or **$5x^{100}$**

questions.answer:
Binomial of degree 35: $x^{35} + 1$
Monomial of degree 100: $x^{100}$
